title: Kotlin List
content:
### Lists in Kotlin

A `List` is an ordered collection of elements. Kotlin provides both immutable (`List`) and mutable (`MutableList`) lists.

---

### Creating Lists

```kotlin
// Immutable lists
val numbers = listOf(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
val empty = emptyList<String>()
val mixed = listOf(1, "Hello", true)  // List<Any>

// Mutable lists
val mutable = mutableListOf(1, 2, 3)
val arrayList = arrayListOf(1, 2, 3)
val buildList = buildList<String> { add("A"); add("B") }
```

---

### Accessing Elements

```kotlin
val list = listOf("a", "b", "c", "d", "e")

println(list[0])         // a
println(list.first())    // a
println(list.last())     // e
println(list.getOrNull(10))  // null (safe access)
println(list.elementAtOrElse(10) { "default" })  // default
```

---

### Common List Operations

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `size` | Number of elements |
| `isEmpty()` | Returns true if empty |
| `contains(element)` | Checks if element exists |
| `indexOf(element)` | First index of element |
| `subList(from, to)` | Returns sublist |
| `reversed()` | Reversed list |
| `sorted()` | Sorted list |

```kotlin
val list = listOf(3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9)
println(list.size)           // 6
println(list.contains(4))    // true
println(list.sorted())       // [1, 1, 3, 4, 5, 9]
println(list.reversed())     // [9, 5, 1, 4, 1, 3]
```

---

### Iterating Over Lists

```kotlin
val items = listOf("A", "B", "C")

for (item in items) {
    println(item)
}

items.forEach { println(it) }

// With index
for ((index, value) in items.withIndex()) {
    println("$index: $value")
}
```

---

### Filtering and Transforming

```kotlin
val numbers = listOf(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6)

val evens = numbers.filter { it % 2 == 0 }       // [2, 4, 6]
val doubled = numbers.map { it * 2 }              // [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12]
val sum = numbers.sum()                            // 21
val firstEven = numbers.first { it % 2 == 0 }      // 2
```

> **Key Point:** Prefer `listOf()` for read-only lists and `mutableListOf()` when modification is needed. Use `getOrNull()` to safely access elements by index.

quiz.question: How do you create an immutable list in Kotlin?
What does getOrNull() return if the index is out of bounds?
